subject: Project Management
title: Humility as a Superpower
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Humility as a Superpower.

hook.text: The best forecasters share one trait above all others: they know they might be wrong.

buildup.text: Intellectual humility means holding your estimates loosely and welcoming challenges to your assumptions.

discovery.text: Humble planners build bigger buffers, seek more feedback, and kill failing projects earlier.

twist.text: In project cultures that reward confidence, humility is punished. The loudest voice wins, not the wisest.

climax.text: The planning fallacy's cure is simple to describe and hard to practice: be honestly uncertain.

punchline.text: The bravest thing a planner can say is 'I don't know.'

quiz.question: Why is intellectual humility valuable in project management?
options: It leads to bigger buffers, better feedback, and earlier kills of failing projects
It makes people feel less stressed
